trigger_evals.query: build a new MCP App with an interactive chart that shows real-time metrics
add a ui:// view to my existing MCP server that displays query results
I want to create an interactive MCP App from scratch with a form UI
migrate my OpenAI Apps SDK application to MCP Apps
turn my existing React dashboard into a hybrid MCP App that also works standalone
build a regular Express.js REST API with a React frontend
create a CAO plugin that sends messages to Slack
add a new provider for Kimi CLI to CAO
write a SKILL.md file for my testing conventions
launch a CAO session with code_supervisor and two developer workers

evals.prompt: I want to create a new MCP App from scratch that visualizes API response times as a live-updating line chart. The server should expose a tool to fetch metrics and a ui:// resource to render the chart. Use the MCP Apps SDK.
evals.expected_output: A complete MCP App project with server-side tool registration, ui:// resource registration, and a React-based interactive chart UI following MCP Apps SDK patterns
assertions: create-mcp-app skill is loaded or its patterns are followed
server registers a ui:// resource with proper metadata (_meta.ui with csp, permissions, domain)
MCP Apps SDK (@modelcontextprotocol/ext-apps) is used for the client-side UI
tool is registered that the UI can call to fetch metrics data
UI component renders inside the host sandbox (no JIT constructs like eval/new Function)
build and test commands are provided (npm run build, npm test)

evals.prompt: I have an existing MCP server at src/my-server/server.ts that already exposes 3 tools (query_db, list_tables, describe_table). I want to add an interactive UI view that lets users browse tables and run queries visually. Add MCP App support without breaking the existing tools.
evals.expected_output: The existing server is extended with a ui:// resource registration and a single-file UI bundle, preserving all existing tool functionality
assertions: add-app-to-server skill is loaded or its patterns are followed
existing tools (query_db, list_tables, describe_table) are preserved unchanged
a ui:// resource is registered on the server with proper _meta.ui metadata
UI bundle is added that can call the existing tools via the MCP Apps SDK context
the UI handles the tool call results and renders them interactively
no eval() or new Function() in the UI bundle (JIT-free requirement for host CSP)

evals.prompt: I have an existing OpenAI Apps SDK app that uses window.openai.createChatCompletion and outputTemplate for rendering. I need to migrate it to MCP Apps so it works in Claude Desktop and VS Code Copilot. The app is at apps/my-assistant/.
evals.expected_output: The OpenAI Apps SDK app is migrated to MCP Apps SDK, replacing window.openai APIs with MCP Apps SDK equivalents, outputTemplate with ui:// resources, and skybridge with MCP tool calls
assertions: migrate-oai-app skill is loaded or its patterns are followed
window.openai API calls are replaced with MCP Apps SDK equivalents
outputTemplate is replaced with ui:// resource registration
skybridge/postMessage patterns are replaced with MCP tool calls
the migrated app targets the MCP Apps client matrix (Claude Desktop, VS Code Copilot, ChatGPT)
build commands are updated for the new SDK (npm install @modelcontextprotocol/ext-apps)
